title: Client-side E2EE for encrypted messaging (WebCrypto AES-GCM + RSA-OAEP)

requirements.summary: Encrypt outgoing ISO 20022 / SWIFT plaintext locally with per-message AES-GCM, wrap the AES key using recipient RSA-OAEP public key, and send only ciphertext + encrypted symmetric key to the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: When sending a message from ComposeMessagePage, the backend sendMessage call is made with encryptedPayload and keyId/encrypted key bytes only (no plaintext is sent).
If the recipient does not have a publicKey registered, the UI blocks sending and shows a clear error in English.
Encryption uses WebCrypto (AES-GCM + RSA-OAEP) and produces different ciphertext for the same plaintext on repeated sends (fresh IV / key per message).

file_operations.description: Align the hybrid crypto helper API to E2EE naming: return and accept `encryptedSymmetricKey` (RSA-OAEP-wrapped per-message AES key) instead of `keyId`; keep AES-GCM per-message key generation and fresh IV behavior; add/adjust inline comments to explicitly state that this is the encrypted symmetric key used for E2EE (not a “vetKey”).

Update the send-message mutation parameter naming to `encryptedSymmetricKey` and pass it to the backend sendMessage call; remove/replace any `keyId` naming so that only ciphertext + encrypted symmetric key are transported from the client.

Ensure ComposeMessagePage generates ISO 20022/SWIFT raw plaintext locally, encrypts it via the E2EE helper, and calls `useSendMessage` with only `encryptedPayload` + `encryptedSymmetricKey` (no plaintext). Strengthen UI blocking when `recipientProfile.publicKey` is missing with a clear English error message; keep the existing sync-status gating so sending is prevented unless encryption prerequisites are satisfied.

requirements.summary: Decrypt received message content client-side using only the locally stored private key and show clear English error states when decryption is not possible or fails.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening a received message in MessageDetailPage decrypts successfully when the recipient has their transport private key available locally.
If the user lacks a local keyPair/privateKey, MessageDetailPage shows an English error state indicating the device cannot decrypt the message.
If a non-recipient attempts to decrypt a message (or ciphertext/key mismatch occurs), the UI shows an English error and does not display any plaintext.

file_operations.description: Update MessageDetailPage decryption flow to use `message.encryptedSymmetricKey` with the local `keyPair.privateKey`. Add an explicit error state when no local transport key/private key is available. Prevent plaintext from rendering on any decryption failure. Optionally gate decryption attempts to received messages (or show an English informational/error state for sent messages that are not decryptable by the sender).
Update `decryptPayload` to accept `encryptedSymmetricKey` naming and ensure decryption failures propagate cleanly so UI can show an English error without exposing any plaintext.

requirements.summary: Clarify naming/intent of the per-message RSA-OAEP encrypted symmetric key field across frontend code so it is not mislabeled as a vetKey, while maintaining interoperability.
acceptanceCriteria: Code identifiers and inline comments no longer imply that the stored per-message encrypted key is a “vetKey” (it is the encrypted symmetric key for E2EE).
Frontend and backend still interoperate after the rename/clarification (send -> store -> list -> decrypt flow works).

file_operations.description: Rename client-side message field handling from `keyId` to `encryptedSymmetricKey` (and update any types/imports accordingly). If needed for compatibility, normalize older message objects by mapping a legacy `keyId` field into `encryptedSymmetricKey` in the query result (without changing stored data), and document via inline comments that the value is the RSA-OAEP encrypted per-message symmetric key used for E2EE decryption.

Update InboxPage to consume the normalized encrypted-message shape (no `keyId` naming assumptions) and keep showing direction badges ('Received'/'Sent') based on `to`/`from` vs current principal, without introducing any frontend workaround calls.

Replace any lingering `keyId` naming in ComposeMessagePage with `encryptedSymmetricKey` and add a brief inline comment clarifying it is the RSA-OAEP encrypted per-message AES key for E2EE (distinct from the session-scoped vetKey used for transport key persistence).

Replace any lingering `keyId` naming in MessageDetailPage with `encryptedSymmetricKey` and adjust inline comments/error text to reflect that decryption uses the locally stored private key to unwrap the per-message symmetric key (not any vetKey).
